checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully represents the text found in the source PDF. The title, date, map labels (extracted as a paragraph), legend categories, sources, and scale information all have a direct basis in the source image. No paragraphs, sentences, or sections were invented by the conversion process.
The source document contains no contact information such as phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. Consequently, no such information appears in the output, and no fabrications were made.

The output contains all key sections from the single-page source document: the main title and date, the map content (represented by an image and extracted labels), the 'Parcel Updates' legend, the 'Sources' section, and the map scale.
There are no formal tables in the source document. The legend information is correctly structured as a list.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant text omissions were identified. The legend items are complete, matching the eight categories in the source. The source information is fully captured. The list of county names extracted from the map is extensive and reflects the labels visible in the graphic.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the source accurately. The grouping of map labels into a single paragraph and the preservation of line breaks in the sources section are acceptable formatting choices that do not alter the informational content of the document.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed both the source PDF and the output JSON. The content appears to be unique throughout the document. The text labels for the counties (OCR'd from the map) appear once as a text block, the legend items are listed once, and the sources/metadata appear once. There are no instances of paragraphs, headings, or list items being repeated unnecessarily.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The main document title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County' is used as the section title (implied H1), and subsequent sub-sections 'Parcel Updates:' and 'Sources:' are correctly labeled as level 2 headings (H2). There are no skipped levels.

There are no tables in the source document. Data is represented visually via a map and a legend, which has been converted to a list. Therefore, this check is not applicable but passes by default as no corrupted table structures exist.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom and left-to-right flow of the source document: Title -> Date -> Map/Image (with county text) -> Legend/Updates -> Sources -> Scale. This accurately reflects the information flow of the original map poster.

The legend in the source document, which lists different quarters and their corresponding status colors, has been appropriately converted into an unordered list in the JSON. The nesting and itemization accurately reflect the legend's structure.
The source document does not contain any URLs or hyperlinks. No links were added or fabricated in the output JSON.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is organized around a spatial thematic map with a legend and logo. The HTML output drastically rearranges this content: the logo is moved from the bottom-right to the top, and the map itself is entirely removed and replaced by a dense, unformatted block of comma-separated text representing the county names extracted via OCR. This collapse from a graphical spatial layout to a single-column text-only layout significantly alters the visual organization and prevents a user from finding information in roughly the same place or manner as the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 shows a thematic map of NC counties as the central visual element, with a legend at the bottom left and a logo at the bottom right.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows the logo at the top, followed by a massive text block of county names. The map graphic is gone, and the spatial relationship of the elements is lost.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no tables; it consists of a map, text blocks, and logos.

The primary meaningful image in the source PDF is the color-coded thematic map of North Carolina. This image is completely absent from the HTML output, replaced only by a text-dump of its labels. Additionally, the legend in the source uses colored squares to visually represent the project status for each quarter; these meaningful icons are missing in the HTML rendering (rendered as a plain bulleted list), leaving only the text labels which are uninterpretable without the colors.
checks.b_evidence_source: The thematic map of NC and the colored square icons in the 'Parcel Updates' legend on page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML rendering lacks the map image and the colored legend icons.

checks.a_analysis: No body content, such as paragraphs or lists, is visually duplicated in the HTML output. The titles and section headers appear once as expected.
